status: DEV — NOT patient-facing; NOT an AU dose source; NOT prescribing guidance. US/EU APPROVED LABEL doses sourced from AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC), stored as what they are: foreign-jurisdiction dose guidance, labelled by jurisdiction. Structurally ISOLATED from the PharmCheck engine (no accessor reads it) so a foreign label can never become an AU dose — the 'Australian healthcare context only' hard limit is preserved by construction, not by wording. Records author to review_status 'draft'.
isolation_note: No PharmDataSource exposes a getInternationalDoseGuidance() accessor and the engine never reads this file. Its only consumers are (a) a clinician reading a foreign label beside the AU dose at the point of decision, and (b) DoseGuidanceSchema.au_congruence, which APPRAISES the AU record against it. The appraisal annotates; it never vetoes an AU dose — an AU dose derives its authority from APF22 / the TGA PI, and letting a foreign regulator veto it would invert the jurisdiction rule.
integrity_bar: A record ships only if it carries a real AMASS RegulatoryCore id (amass_id) for a specific authorisation, plus the approved INDICATION the label dose applies to — because a differing indication is the usual, legitimate reason an AU dose differs from a US/EU one. Label FACTS + citation only: FDA SPL is US public domain and EMA SmPC is © EMA, so no SPL/SmPC prose is reproduced here (the same facts-and-citation basis that governs apf22).

attestation.statement: US/EU label dose guidance — reference only, engine-isolated, NEVER an AU dose. Clinical sign-off is NOT required for this register to be USEFUL (it is foreign regulatory fact, not Breath-Ezy clinical judgement), but it is required before any record is surfaced to a clinician as decision-support context. Empty skeleton — no records authored yet (C1 populates it from AMASS).
attestation.scope: pharm-international-dose-guidance dev v0.1.0 (skeleton — no records authored yet)

records.context: Treatment of adults with rheumatoid arthritis
records.dose_statement: The recommended starting dosage of JYLAMVO is 7.5 mg orally once weekly with escalation to achieve optimal response. Dosages of more than 20 mg once weekly result in an increased risk of serious adverse reactions, including myelosuppression.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Active rheumatoid arthritis in adult patients
records.dose_statement: The recommended initial dose is 7.5 mg (3.75 ml) methotrexate once weekly.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Treatment of patients with acute manic or mixed episodes associated with bipolar I disorder
records.dose_statement: The recommended initial dose of EQUETRO is 200 mg administered twice daily. The dose may be increased by 200 mg per day to achieve optimal clinical response.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus
records.dose_statement: The recommended starting dose of GLUMETZA is 500 mg orally once daily with the evening meal. Increase the dose in increments of 500 mg every 1 to 2 weeks on the basis of glycemic control and tolerability, up to a maximum of 2,000 mg once daily with the evening meal.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Treatment of mild to moderate ulcerative colitis, and adjunctive therapy in severe ulcerative colitis (Initial Therapy)
records.dose_statement: Adults: 3 to 4 g daily in evenly divided doses with dosage intervals not exceeding eight hours. It may be advisable to initiate therapy with a lower dosage, e.g., 1 to 2 g daily, to reduce possible gastrointestinal intolerance.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Treatment of tonic-clonic (grand mal) and psychomotor (temporal lobe) seizures
records.dose_statement: The recommended starting dosage for adult patients who have received no previous treatment is 5 mL (125 mg/5 mL), or one teaspoonful, by mouth three times daily. Adjust the dosage to suit individual requirements, up to a maximum of 25 mL daily.

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Treatment of osteoporosis in postmenopausal women
records.dose_statement: The recommended dosage is: one 70 mg tablet once weekly or one bottle of 70 mg oral solution once weekly or one 10 mg tablet once daily

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Reduction of risk of stroke and systemic embolism in patients with nonvalvular atrial fibrillation
records.dose_statement: The recommended dose of ELIQUIS for most patients is 5 mg taken orally twice daily. The recommended dose of ELIQUIS is 2.5 mg twice daily in patients with at least two of the following characteristics: age greater than or equal to 80 years, body weight less than or equal to 60 kg, serum creatinine greater than or equal to 1.5 mg/dL

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Prevention of stroke and systemic embolism in adult patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation (NVAF) with one or more risk factors
records.dose_statement: The recommended dose of apixaban is 5 mg taken orally twice daily. Dose reduction: The recommended dose of apixaban is 2.5 mg taken orally twice daily in patients with NVAF and at least two of the following characteristics: age >= 80 years, body weight <= 60 kg, or serum creatinine >= 1.5 mg/dL (133 micromole/L).
records.authorisation_name: Apixaban Accord (generic; Eliquis EMA 4.2 exposes only paediatric granules)

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)

records.context: Reduction of risk of stroke and systemic embolism in adult patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation
records.dose_statement: For patients with creatinine clearance (CrCl) > 30 mL/min, the recommended dosage of PRADAXA Capsules is 150 mg taken orally, twice daily. For patients with severe renal impairment (CrCl 15-30 mL/min), the recommended dosage of PRADAXA Capsules is 75 mg twice daily

provenance.source: AMASS RegulatoryCore (FDA SPL / EMA SmPC label facts, cited)
provenance.authored_by: claude-fable-5 AMASS retrieval workflow (verbatim section extraction; no dose written from memory)
